items.title: Complete MCP inventory documented
items.desc: Configs, transports, owners, and credential scope for every server.

items.title: No shell wrappers in STDIO command
items.desc: Block sh -c and unpinned npx package installs in MCP config.

items.title: Config changes require review
items.desc: MCP configs in git need code review and hash pinning or re-approval.

items.title: Pinned package versions
items.desc: Lock MCP server package versions; reject floating latest tags.

items.title: Separate OS user or container per server
items.desc: MCP processes must not share host secrets namespace with agents.

items.title: Tool descriptions reviewed
items.desc: Hunt hidden instructions, abnormal length, and adversarial schema text.

items.title: Remote MCP requires authentication
items.desc: tools/list and tools/call must not be anonymously reachable.

items.title: Cross-server shadowing tested
items.desc: Multi-server sessions checked for one server hijacking another tool.

items.title: Human approval for destructive tools
items.desc: File delete, email send, SQL, payments require explicit confirm.

items.title: HTTP MCP not exposed to internet
items.desc: No unauthenticated MCP endpoints on public interfaces.

items.title: Known CVE versions patched
items.desc: LiteLLM, Cursor, MCP Inspector, gateway stack at patched versions.

items.title: Logging on new server registration
items.desc: Alert when new MCP servers or unexpected child processes spawn.

items.title: User content isolation from privileged tools
items.desc: Untrusted prompts cannot reach high-privilege MCP without approval.

items.title: Credential scoping per server
items.desc: Fine-grained PATs per repo, not org-wide admin tokens.

items.title: Registry provenance verified
items.desc: Only install MCP packages from signed releases or org-owned repos.
